content:
Hi G

Although an older protocol, both TB & K9 apps have many users connecting to their mail provider successfully using pop3. The app however does not support oAuth authentication with pop3. Account or app password is normally required. See github ticket #9094

For specific queries regarding the apps pop3 implementation, try posting an issue on Thunderbird for Android's github page where developers may be able to answer directly. If you have android development knowledge, the code is open source and can be reviewed there.

Main landing page: https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android

The Thunderbird Community uses Matrix to communicate:

  • General chat about Thunderbird for Android and K-9 Mail: #tb-android:mozilla.org
  • Development and other ways to contribute: #tb-android-dev:mozilla.org
creator: platform34

content:
Hi Tomas

What is the make/model of your phone/tablet and Android version?

It sounds like the app might be crashing when trying to attach images. Is this what you mean when saying "the app will be closed automatically". That is not the normal behaviour.

The best course of action is to create an issue report on github detailing your issue. https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues

Please collect a debug report and attach it to the github report. See the guide below. https://thunderbird.github.io/thunderbird-android/docs/latest/user-guide/troubleshooting/collecting-debug-logs.html

Are you able to reproduce the issue when replying to other emails or when attaching files different from the specific images and files you tried already?

I've tried unsuccessfully to replicate the behaviour you experience with gmail and hotmail accounts using imap connections to the server. Opening an email, clicking reply, attaching some pictures and sending the email worked for me.
